scene.description: The observatory is not a place anymore — it's three nights stacked into one. All layers exist at once. Shifting between them doesn't change the memory. It changes which version of you is looking.

fragments.label: Layer A — Calm Observation
fragments.text: Layer A: a quiet night at the telescope. Someone takes a reading and writes it down. Nothing is wrong yet.

fragments.label: Layer B — Signal Disruption
fragments.text: Layer B: the same instruments scream. A signal that shouldn't exist, timestamped from a night that hasn't happened.

fragments.label: Layer C — Aftermath
fragments.text: Layer C: the room is empty. The observers are gone — not dead, unrendered. The chairs are still warm.

fragments.label: The Observer Is You
fragments.symbol: You notice the version of yourself reading each layer is different. Layer A's you is calm. Layer C's you already knows how it ends.

fragments.label: The Observatory Logbook
fragments.text: An entry in the log, dated to Layer B, in your handwriting: 'Signal confirmed. Source is internal. Do not tell the next one.' You have no memory of writing it.

fragments.label: The Empty Chair
fragments.text: In Layer C, one chair is pulled out as if someone just stood. It's the observer's chair. It's still warm — and it's the same temperature in all three layers at once.

interpretations.label: Multi-iteration bleed — your other selves are the signal
